description: Program 1 baseline hypotheses. These witnesses, when confirmed at finite precision, suggest finite/proxy lemmas bearing on the Same-Object Certificate, NC3, and NC4. Confirmation never proves the base claim; it preserves the route.

hypotheses.plain_statement: The harmonic converter / explicit-formula reconstruction behaves covariantly across the tested gauge scales k.

expected_signature.pass_rule: drift remains within tolerance for every tested k

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Reconstruction covariance is the finite-precision proxy for the same-object behavior the gauge claims. Without it, NC3 (same-case criterion) cannot be witnessed by reconstruction agreement.
failure_means: current reconstruction-covariance baseline was not confirmed in this run
the route via reconstruction agreement may need a different metric
the implementation, the precision, or the test window may be wrong
possible_alternative_hypotheses: covariance holds only for a restricted k-range
covariance holds only with specific tau / dps settings
reconstruction agreement is the wrong proxy and a different invariant is needed
intended_inference_if_passed: the finite reconstruction-covariance proxy is consistent with same-object behavior on the tested window
this preserves the candidate route to NC3 via reconstruction agreement
no formal claim about same-object equality is established
disallowed_conclusions: this turns the proxy into a formal proof

hypotheses.plain_statement: The recovered beta parameter remains pinned at 1/2 across the tested gauge scales.

expected_signature.tolerance: adaptive precision tolerance for this run
expected_signature.pass_rule: |recovered_beta - 0.5| within declared tolerance for every tested k

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Beta stability is the finite-precision proxy for predicate-preservation under the gauge. It is the closest experiment to NC4 (predicate transport).
failure_means: the predicate-preservation proxy is threatened in this window
the current adaptive tolerance may be too tight or wrong
the recovery routine may have an implementation bug
possible_alternative_hypotheses: beta drifts within a bounded envelope rather than being pinned
beta stability holds only for k near zero
the measurement is dominated by precision floor
intended_inference_if_passed: predicate-preservation proxy is consistent on the tested window
the route to NC4 via beta stability is preserved
this is not a proof of NC4

hypotheses.plain_statement: Scaled-zero / scaled-lattice equivalence holds within adaptive tolerance over the tested k-range.

expected_signature.expected_value: within adaptive tolerance for every tested k
expected_signature.pass_rule: scaled-zero correspondence holds for every tested k under tolerance

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Scaled-zero correspondence is the witness object for NC3 via zero-ensemble equivalence. Without it, the predicate-transport route loses its finite witness.
failure_means: the current zero-scaling witness is threatened on the tested window
the chosen correspondence metric may be too strong
the chosen tolerance may be inappropriate
failure_does_not_mean: exact predicate transport is refuted (unless this witness is shown to be necessary)
possible_alternative_hypotheses: correspondence holds modulo a small structured residual
a different distance metric better captures the equivalence
intended_inference_if_passed: the zero-ensemble equivalence proxy is consistent on the tested window
NC3 route via zero-ensemble is preserved
this is not a proof of NC3
